pr.title: Use Gm_PKind enums for player SlotType return value
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Replaced magic numeric slot-type values returned by Player_GetPlayerSlotType and passed to Player_SetSlottype with named Gm_PKind enum constants across fighter boss code, game-mode logic, status UI, Stadium, trophy/demo viewer setup, and utility code. The bot reported no GALE01 code changes, so this was a readability/type-semantic cleanup that preserved matching.
what_changed: Converted many Player_GetPlayerSlotType(...) == 0 comparisons to == Gm_PKind_Human, especially in Crazy Hand IASA callbacks and Master Hand poke IASA where human-controlled boss input gates ftBossLib_8015BD20.
Converted slot-type checks for active non-empty slots from != 3 to != Gm_PKind_NA in gm_16AE.c, gm_17C0.c, grstadium.c, and un_2FC91.c.
Converted human-or-CPU checks from == 0 || == 1 to == Gm_PKind_Human || == Gm_PKind_Cpu in camera/status related game-mode and interface paths.
Converted Player_SetSlottype(..., 3) to Player_SetSlottype(..., Gm_PKind_NA) for clearing inactive slots.
Converted Player_SetSlottype(..., 2) to Player_SetSlottype(..., Gm_PKind_Demo) in demo/trophy/video setup paths such as gmregtyfall.c, vi0102.c, and vi0502.c.
Made one implicit truthiness test explicit in un_2FC91.c: Player_GetPlayerSlotType(i) became Player_GetPlayerSlotType(i) != Gm_PKind_Human.
Some formatting changed alongside the enum substitutions, including line wrapping and cast spacing in grstadium.c and ifstatus.c.
smart_moves: Applied the enum conversion broadly instead of leaving mixed magic numbers, making Player slot-kind semantics consistent across subsystems.
Preserved behavior by mapping known numeric values directly to enum names: 0 to Gm_PKind_Human, 1 to Gm_PKind_Cpu, 2 to Gm_PKind_Demo, and 3 to Gm_PKind_NA.
Kept comparison structure largely unchanged, reducing risk of codegen or control-flow drift.
Used explicit enum comparisons to document intent in places where the same return value distinguishes human input, CPU participants, demo actors, and unused slots.
Verified matching through the decomp.dev bot report, which stated GALE01 had no changes.
decomp_lessons: When an accessor has an enum-valued domain, replace raw literals at call sites with the enum constants once the enum is known; this improves future reasoning without changing generated code.
For slot-type cleanup, audit both getters and setters: Player_GetPlayerSlotType comparisons and Player_SetSlottype assignments should use the same Gm_PKind vocabulary.
Avoid relying on truthiness for enum returns when the intent is to exclude a specific enum member; spell out comparisons such as != Gm_PKind_Human.
Use enum names to distinguish semantically different cases that were previously only numbers: human, CPU, demo, and not available.
Small semantic cleanups can span many subsystems because player slot type is a cross-cutting concept used by fighter action callbacks, match rules, UI status, stage effects, and demo setup.
naming_conventions: Use Gm_PKind_Human for slot type value 0.
Use Gm_PKind_Cpu for slot type value 1.
Use Gm_PKind_Demo for slot type value 2.
Use Gm_PKind_NA for slot type value 3 / inactive or unavailable slots.
Keep existing Player_GetPlayerSlotType and Player_SetSlottype API names; this PR changed call-site constants, not API spelling.
Slot-type enum constants use the Gm_PKind_* prefix and PascalCase suffixes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Enum constants were substituted for equivalent integer literals, which should compile to the same immediate values.
The bot comment reported: "Report for GALE01 (2559aa0 - 2259b57): No changes", supporting that the enumification was matching-safe.
Most edits preserved surrounding boolean expressions and branch structure, minimizing risk of register allocation or branch changes.
Formatting-only adjustments appeared in a few hunks, but no evidence indicates they affected codegen.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was from decomp-dev[bot], reporting no GALE01 changes.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining Player_GetPlayerSlotType comparisons against raw literals 0, 1, 2, or 3.
Find remaining Player_SetSlottype calls passing raw numeric slot-kind values.
Audit other player slot APIs for enum return types that still use implicit truthiness or magic numbers.
Check whether Gm_PKind enum type can be propagated into function signatures or struct fields related to slot type.
